item.title: Liverpool rocked by last-gasp defeat at Wolves
item.description:
Source: FMT Sports (Sports)
The reigning champions suffer an embarrassing 2-1 stoppage-time defeat at the Premier League's bottom club.
Matched: war(+3), ai(+3), who(+2), malaysia(+3), premier league(+2), champions league(+2), liverpool(+1), chelsea(+1)

item.title: India’s Sindhu withdraws from All England Open after Dubai ordeal
item.description:
Source: FMT Sports (Sports)
PV Sindhu was stranded at Dubai airport on her way to the All England Open as global air travel was disrupted since Sunday after the US and Israel attacked Iran.
Matched: war(+3), ai(+3), launch(+2), malaysia(+3), bwf(+2), thailand(+1), olympic(+2), badminton(+2)

item.title: World Cup ticket frenzy unfolds amid challenges
item.description:
Source: FMT Sports (Sports)
World Cup 2026 ticket sales were oversubscribed 30 times, despite global unrest and high prices deterring fans.
Matched: policy(+3), budget(+3), war(+3), ai(+3), malaysia(+3), aff(+2), football(+1)

item.title: Jun Hao shocks Nishimoto in All England opener
item.description:
Source: FMT Sports (Sports)
Overcoming his first-round jinx, the 26th-ranked Malaysian player beat the higher-ranked Japanese shuttler in three games to progress to the second round.

item.title: 5 things about the 2026 F1 season

item.title: Home comfort at last for Everton, Leeds beaten by Sunderland
item.description:
Source: FMT Sports (Sports)
Everton end a seven-match home winless run by beating Burnley 2-0 as relegation fears deepen for Leeds after a 1-0 defeat.

item.title: ‘Safety and wellbeing’ will guide F1 Mideast planning, says FIA chief

item.title: Real Madrid superstar Mbappe in Paris for treatment on knee injury

Source: FMT Sports (Sports)

item.title: Juve stay in Champions League hunt with last-gasp Roma draw
item.description:

item.title: Arteta embraces Arsenal’s ‘Set-Piece FC’ label after corners sink Chelsea
item.description:
Source: FMT Sports (Sports)

item.title: 'We'd forgotten how to win' - Edwards on 'enjoyable' victory
item.description:
Source: BBC Sport (Sports)
Wolves manager Rob Edwards praises his sides commitment to improvement, but insists they're not thinking about staying in the Premier League, despite beating Liverpool 2-1 at Molineux to give them back-to-back league wins for the first time this season.

item.title: Ronaldo injured but should be fit for World Cup
